hook.text: Construction wastes 57% of labor time — workers waiting, walking, looking for materials.

buildup.text: Lean construction applies Toyota's principles: reduce waste, improve flow, deliver only what's needed.

discovery.text: The Last Planner System lets frontline workers plan their own work weekly, reducing delays by 30%.

twist.text: An industry that hasn't changed in centuries is finally adopting ideas from a 1950s car factory.

climax.text: Lean construction is proof that even the oldest industries can benefit from modern flow thinking.

punchline.text: Hard hats are finally learning from factory floors.

quiz.question: How much labor time does construction typically waste?
